questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Consider the following ligands $\mathrm{NH}_2$, acac;, $\mathrm{OH}^{-}$, gly, $\mathrm{O}_2^{-}$, o-Phen, $\mathrm{Hdmg}, \mathrm{NO}_2^{-}, \mathrm{CO}_3^{2-}, \mathrm{Cl}^{-}, \mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{COO}^{-}$, en $\mathrm{SO}_4^{2-}$ Then calculate value of "P + Q-R - S"
Here
P : Total numbers of ligands which act as bridging as well as monodentate only
Q : Total number of flexidentate ligands
$R$ : Total number of bidentate ligands only
S : Total number of unsymmetrical bidentate ligands
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Consider the following complex compounds
(i) $\left[\mathrm{Cu}\left(\mathrm{NH}_3\right)_4\right)\left[\mathrm{Cu}\left(\mathrm{NO}_2\right)_4\right]$
(ii) $\left[\mathrm{Cr}(\mathrm{py})_6\right)\left[\mathrm{Cr}(\mathrm{SCN})_6\right]$
(iii) $\left[\mathrm{Co}\left(\mathrm{NH}_3\right)_5\left(\mathrm{NO}_2\right)\right]\left[\mathrm{Pt}(\mathrm{SCN})_4\right]$

If $x_1, x_2$, and $x_3$ are more possible coordination isomers of given complex compounds respectively, then calculate the value of $\left(x_1\right)^2+\left(x_2\right)^2+\left(x_3\right)^2$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Number of unpaired electrons in compound $\left[\mathrm{Mn}(\mathrm{CN})_6\right]^{3-}$ is:
questions.solution:
$\left[\mathrm{Mn}(\mathrm{CN})_6\right]^{3-}$ $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{x}+6(-1)=-3 \\ & \therefore \mathrm{x}=+3\end{aligned}$ $\mathrm{Mn}^{3+} \longrightarrow[\mathrm{Ar}] 3 \mathrm{~d}^4 4 \mathrm{~S}^0$
As $\mathrm{CN}^{-}$is strong ligand hence due to splitting it will from low spin complex.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: How many type of isomerism is exhibited by the complex $\left[\mathrm{Co}\left(\mathrm{NH}_3\right)_4\left(\mathrm{NO}_2\right)_2\right] \mathrm{Cl}$ ?
questions.solution:
(i) Ionisation isomers $\left[\mathrm{Co}\left(\mathrm{NH}_3\right)_4\left(\mathrm{NO}_2\right)_2\right] \mathrm{C} \ell$ and $\left[\mathrm{Co}\left(\mathrm{NH}_3\right)_4\left(\mathrm{NO}_2\right)(\mathrm{C} \ell)\right] \mathrm{NO}_2$
(ii) Linkage isomers $\left[\mathrm{Co}\left(\mathrm{NH}_3\right)_4\left(\mathrm{NO}_2\right)_2\right] \mathrm{C} \ell$ and $\left[\mathrm{Co}\left(\mathrm{NH}_3\right)_4(\mathrm{ONO})_2\right] \mathrm{C} \ell$
(iii) Geometrical isomers ; cis \& trans
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
How many complexes will show optical but not geometrical isomerism?
$\left[\mathrm{Pt}(\mathrm{en})_2 \mathrm{Cl}_2\right]^{2+}, \quad\left[\mathrm{Pt}(\mathrm{Py}) \mathrm{NH}_3 \mathrm{ClBr}\right]$
$\left[\mathrm{Pt}(\mathrm{en})(\mathrm{ox}) \mathrm{Cl}_2\right],\left[\mathrm{Pt}(\mathrm{en})_3\right]^{4+}$
questions.solution: $\left[\mathrm{Pt}(\mathrm{en})_3\right]^{4+}$ is optically active but does not show geometrical isomerism.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
(i) Ti is purified by Van Arkel method. In it formula of metal iodide vapour formed is TiI $_{\mathrm{p}}$.
(ii) Oxidation state of Fe in Fool's gold is q.
(iii) In the test of $\mathrm{Ni}^{2+}$ rosy red ppt. of $\left[\mathrm{Ni}(\mathrm{dmg})_2\right]$ is formed.

In it r five membered rings and s six membered. H-bonding having ring are formed.
Value of $\left(\frac{p+q+r+s}{2}\right)$ is........
questions.solution: (i) Van Arkel method $\underset{\text { (vapour) }}{\mathrm{Ti}}+2 \mathrm{I}_2 \xrightarrow{\Delta} \underset{\text { (vapour) }}{\mathrm{TiI}_4}$ $\xrightarrow[\text { high temperature }]{\Delta} \underset{\text { (pure) }}{\mathrm{Ti}}+2 \mathrm{I}_2$ (ii) Fools gold $\mathrm{FeS}_2 \longrightarrow \mathrm{Fe}^{2+} \mathrm{S}^{-}-\mathrm{S}^{-}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
How many of the following contain all or some iron in +2 oxidation state?
(a) $\mathrm{FeS}_2$
(b) Haematite
(c) Magnetite
(d) Brown ring complex
(e) $\mathrm{Na}_2\left[\mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{CN})_5(\mathrm{NO})\right]$
(f) $\mathrm{Fe}\left[\mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{CN})_6\right]$
(g) $\mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{Fe}\left[\mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{CN})_6\right]$
(h) Ferrocene $\left(\left[\mathrm{Fe}\left(\mathrm{C}_5 \mathrm{H}_5\right)_2\right]\right)$
(i) $\mathrm{FeWO}_4$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
How many of the following are optically inactive?
i. trans-Coen2NH3Cl2+
ii. cis-[Co(en)2Br2]+
iii. [Co(NH3)3Cl3]
iv. trans-CoNH34Cl2+
v. trans-[CoCl2(C2O4)2]3-
questions.solution: Complexes <math><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>,</mo><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>,</mo><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">v</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> and <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">v</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> are optically inactive due to the presence of plane of symmetry.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If for complex $\left[\mathrm{Cr}\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)_6\right]^{2+} \mathrm{CFSE}$ is $\left(-\mathrm{R} \Delta_0\right)$ calculated from general formula of CFSE than determine value of 100 R .
questions.solution:
${ }_{24} \mathrm{Cr}^{2+}=[\mathrm{Ar}] 3 \mathrm{~d}^4$ (as $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ is WFE Ligand). Therefore ${ }_{24} \mathrm{Cr}^{2+}$ is
$\mathrm{t}_{2 \mathrm{~g}}^{1,1,1}, e_{\mathrm{g}}^{1,0}$
$\begin{aligned} & \text { or CFSE }=(-3 \times 0.4+1 \times 0.6) \Delta_0 \\ & \quad=-0.6 \Delta_0 .\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
